title: Augment With Neutralization And Weather Regime Features
summary: Public F1 data sources expose track status, race-control messages, telemetry, and weather, all of which directly alter pit economics.
rationale: Public F1 data sources expose track status, race-control messages, telemetry, and weather, all of which directly alter pit economics. Your current table has no explicit safety-car, VSC, or rainfall state, so it likely conflates very different strategic regimes.
implementation_hint: Join FastF1 session data to each lap and add fold-safe features such as current flag state, laps since SC/VSC started or ended, restart lap indicator, rainfall, track temperature, and recent weather deltas. Start with coarse binary regime flags before adding richer telemetry-derived summaries.
expected_effect: Should help especially on outlier laps where pit timing is driven by neutralizations or weather shifts rather than tyre wear alone.
risk: Depends on competition rules allowing external data, and joins must be aligned to lap-local timestamps only; race-control events after the lap are leakage.
